title: Creamy Fluffy Canadian Shrimp Bisque
description: A rich, nostalgic Canadian bisque with tender shrimp, velvety cream, and a gentle pressure‑cooking technique that yields a fluffy, comforting soup.

ingredients.name: herbs (dried thyme + bay leaf)
ingredients.notes: 1 tsp dried thyme and 1 bay leaf

ingredients.name: seasoning (salt + pepper)
ingredients.notes: 1/2 tsp sea salt and 1/4 tsp black pepper

instructions: Set your pressure cooker to sauté mode and melt the butter.
Add the diced vegetables and cook until they soften and become translucent, about 4‑5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the flour, stirring constantly to form a smooth roux; cook for 1 minute to eliminate raw flour taste.
Add the chicken broth, diced tomatoes, herbs, and seasoning; stir well and bring to a simmer.
Return the raw shrimp to the pot, seal the pressure cooker, and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure by carefully venting the cooker, ensuring the shrimp reach an internal temperature of 145°F.
Remove the bay leaf, then whisk in the heavy cream and lemon juice; adjust salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped dill if desired.
